sanskrit_devanagari:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः ।
कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥ १५ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
evaṁ jñātvā kṛtaṁ karma pūrvair api mumukṣubhiḥ |
kuru karmaiva tasmāt tvaṁ pūrvaiḥ pūrvataraṁ kṛtam || 15 ||

translations.verbatim_quote: All the liberated souls in ancient times acted with this understanding of My transcendental nature. Therefore you should perform your duty, following in their footsteps.
All the liberated souls in ancient times acted with this understanding of My transcendental nature. Therefore you should perform your duty, following in their footsteps.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 1557 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: There are two classes of men. Some of them are full of polluted material things within their hearts, and some of them are materially free. Kṛṣṇa consciousness is equally beneficial for both of these persons.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.15 Jnatva, having known; evam, thus, that ‘I am not an agent; I have no desire for the results of actions’; karma, duties; krtam, were undertaken; api, even; purvaih, by the ancient; mumuksubhih, seekers of Liberation. Tasmat, therefore; tvam, you; kuru, undertake; karma, action; eva, itself.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-15-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara reads the verse as the directive that follows from the doctrine: knowing right-action does not bind, perform action as the liberated ancients did. The verse explicitly forbids inaction-as-renunciation; karma is to be done, not abandoned, in the manner the ancients did.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 4.15 Actions of the kind described above were performed even by the aspirants of old for liberation, who have become free from evil after knowing Me in this way. Therefore, after having got rid of the sins by knowledge of Me in the aforesaid way, perform actions in the same way as they were performe…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-4-15-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja reads the verse as authoritative: the practice of karma-yoga in the manner the ancients (Vivasvān, Manu, Ikṣvāku, the rājarṣis) practiced it — engaged action without longing for fruits — is what Arjuna is being told to do. The lineage is the model; the action is the practice.

positions.rendering: as performed by the ancients in days of yore
as the liberation-seeking ancients performed it, knowing right-action does not bind
in the manner Vivasvān, Manu, and the royal sages practiced karma-yoga across generations

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All three read pūrvataram as anchoring the directive in lineage-practice. The verse closes the chapter's first major movement by connecting the avatar doctrine back to the original paramparā opening (4.1-4.3). The instruction is to do the practice the lineage already did.

literal_meaning: Knowing thus, action was performed by the ancients seeking liberation; therefore you also perform action, as performed by the ancients in earlier times.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna closes the avatar block by pointing back to the disciplic tradition: the ancients (pūrvaiḥ) who sought liberation (mumukṣubhiḥ) performed action knowing what 4.14 has just established — that right-action does not bind. The instruction is: act as they acted. The verse re-grounds the chapter's teaching in lineage, completing the arc opened at 4.1-4.3 (paramparā) and now expressed as a directive: do as the lineage did.
